title: VIP / High-Profile Patient — Elevated Access Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup vip_patients.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT vip_level, start_date, end_date
| where isnotnull(vip_level) AND _time>=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d") AND _time<=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup vip_care_team.csv PAT_ID USER_ID OUTPUT is_authorized
| where isnull(is_authorized)
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, vip_level, Activity
value: Applies extra scrutiny to any user accessing patients on the VIP/high-profile flag list to deter and detect snooping.
implementation: (1) VIP list restricted to small privacy group managing lookup; (2) real-time alert to privacy on-call; (3) never place plaintext celebrity names in Splunk—use surrogate IDs only.
visualization: Table (masked IDs only), Single value (unauthorized VIP touches).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
